sections.heading: What is WorkManager?
sections.content: WorkManager is Android Jetpack’s library for deferrable, guaranteed background work. It ensures tasks run even if the app is killed or the device restarts. Unlike coroutines or Services, it handles lifecycle and system constraints automatically, making it ideal for tasks like syncing data, uploading logs, or periodic maintenance.

sections.heading: When to Use WorkManager
items: Upload logs or analytics when network is available
Sync data periodically or after connectivity restores
Process images or media files in background
Chain dependent tasks sequentially or in parallel
Apply constraints: network, charging, idle, storage

sections.heading: Core Components
items: WorkInfo — observe task status and output

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Keep Data <10KB; long work >10 min requires foreground
Avoid exact-time tasks — use AlarmManager if needed
Observe WorkInfo in UI, not inside Worker
Use Hilt for dependency injection via @HiltWorker
